title: An optical arrangement for use in an optical coherent detection system
abstract: An optical arrangement for use in a coherent detection system and a method for using same, are described. The optical arrangement comprises: a light beam generating source; an optical sensor, preferably an optical sensor that comprises a frequency selective surface (FSS); and an optical transmitter. The arrangement is characterized in that: a) all optical energy generated by the light beam generating source is directed to the optical sensor for use as a reference light signal thereat, and b) at least part of the optical energy reflected from the optical sensor is forwarded towards the optical transmitter.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An optical arrangement for use in a coherent detection system, wherein said arrangement comprises: a light beam generating source; an optical sensor; and an optical transmitter, and wherein the optical arrangement is characterized in that: a) all optical energy generated by said light beam generating source is directed to said optical sensor for use as a reference light signal thereat, and b) at least part of the optical energy reflected from the optical sensor is directed towards the said optical transmitter. 2. The optical arrangement of claim 1, wherein the optical energy reflected from the optical sensor and received by the optical transmitter is emitted from said optical transmitter in a pre-defined pattern, in order to illuminate a pre-defined field of view. 3. The optical arrangement of claim 1, wherein the said optical sensor comprises a frequency selective surface (FSS) . 4. The optical arrangement of claim 3, wherein the FSS is designed to enable reflecting optical energy therefrom towards the optical transmitter, in a pre-defined pattern. 5. The optical arrangement of claim 3, wherein the optical sensor comprises a plurality of nano rectifying antennas for detecting optical energy received thereat. 6. The optical arrangement of claim 5, wherein said plurality of nano rectifying antennas are operative at near to far IR range of frequencies. 7. The optical arrangement of claim 1, wherein the optical sensor is a photodiode. 8. The optical arrangement of claim, wherein the FSS of the optical sensor comprises an array of different FSS unit cells, where each of said unit cells has its own characteristic phase shift surface. 9. The optical arrangement of claim 1, further comprising a beam shaping means configured to operate on the reference light signal, to enable matching a light beam shape with that of the optical sensor shape. 10. The optical arrangement of claim 1, wherein the illumination source is a gas laser. 11. The optical arrangement of claim 1, wherein said optical sensor comprises at least one array of sensing elements. 12. A method for detecting an optical signal in a coherent detection system, wherein said method comprises the steps of: generating a modulated light beam; directing all of the modulated optical energy generated by a light source to an optical sensor that comprises at least one 

array of sensing elements, for use as a reference light signal thereat; forwarding modulated optical energy reflected from the optical sensor towards an optical transmitter; emitting the light received at the optical transmitter to illuminate a pre-defined field of view; receiving a reflected signal from at least one target located within the pre-defined field of view; and comparing the received signal with the reference light signal, thereby obtaining a coherent detection of the at least one target, and wherein the step of directing all of the modulated optical energy generated by a light source, comprises directing all said optical energy to scan said optical sensor, to ensure that at least a portion of said optical sensor or a sub-array of the at least one array of sensing elements, is illuminated while said optical sensor is operative; and directing at least part of optical energy reflected from the at least a portion of said optical sensor or a sub-array of the at least one array of sensing elements, towards said optical transmitter, to enable illuminating at least a portion of a field of view of a target. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising a step of combining results obtained from a plurality of scanning operations, in order to obtain a unified result.
